title: Measured Viewport Ladder
prompt:
Create a production-ready Measured Viewport Ladder using React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. Build a compact native-scroll reading preview with three semantic chapters beside a sticky black ladder. The mint rung indicates the chapter currently in view. Use a light Bento system: #F4F5F6 surrounding space, white content panels, #111111 text, #4A4A4F support copy, #ECECEE borders, 22px radii, and soft shadows. Do not use gradients on cards.

Keep native scrolling intact: do not intercept wheel, touch, selection, or page input. Animate chapter entry with a 220ms opacity and 10px transform; move the active mint rung by 4px using a spring with stiffness 300 and damping 26. A focusable scroll region needs a clear accessible label and visible focus outline. Each chapter must be an explicitly labelled section, while the ladder remains aria-hidden decoration. Announce chapter changes politely without spamming users.

At 320px, preserve the narrow rail and readable content; at desktop, keep the two-column rhythm. It must work in a compact preview and a large detail panel without fixed viewport assumptions. Preserve contrast and keyboard scrolling. Respect prefers-reduced-motion with static chapters and an unmoving rung. Do not use remote media, random values, storage, network calls, or timers. Return complete TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion client code, plus a runnable pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.
demoCode:

{["Context","Evidence","Decision"].map((_,i)=>)}
